-- Personalized Feed: For You + Interest-Based Explore
-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
-- Safe to re-run (uses CREATE OR REPLACE / IF NOT EXISTS)

-- 1. Add interests_embedding column to users
ALTER TABLE public.users
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS interests_embedding vector(384);

-- 2. Create topic_embeddings lookup table (canonical topics from onboarding)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.topic_embeddings (
    topic TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    embedding vector(384) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- 3. IVFFlat index on users.interests_embedding for fast cosine similarity
-- (lists=10 is fine for < 50k users; bump to sqrt(n) when user base grows)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_interests_embedding
ON public.users
USING ivfflat (interests_embedding vector_cosine_ops)
WITH (lists = 10);

-- 4. RPC: get_for_you_feed
-- Cosine similarity between user's interests_embedding and journal embeddings
-- Composite score: 80% semantic + 10% engagement + 10% recency (90-day window)
-- Excludes own posts & reposts, minimum 0.30 similarity floor, cursor pagination
DO $$ BEGIN
    DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_for_you_feed(UUID, INT, TIMESTAMPTZ);
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN NULL;
END $$;
DO $$ BEGIN
    DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_for_you_feed(UUID, INT, INT);
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN NULL;
END $$;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_for_you_feed(
    p_user_id UUID,
    p_limit INT DEFAULT 10,
    p_offset INT DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    user_id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    content JSONB,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    privacy TEXT,
    views BIGINT,
    is_repost BOOLEAN,
    repost_source_journal_id UUID,
    repost_caption TEXT,
    prompt_id INTEGER,
    user_obj_id UUID,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    like_count BIGINT,
    reaction_count BIGINT,
    comment_count BIGINT,
    bookmark_count BIGINT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    user_embedding vector(384);
    max_hot INT;
BEGIN
    -- Get user's interests embedding
    SELECT u.interests_embedding
    INTO user_embedding
    FROM public.users u
    WHERE u.id = p_user_id;

    -- If no embedding, return empty
    IF user_embedding IS NULL THEN
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL
      AND j2.created_at >= now() - interval '90 days';

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        j.id,
        j.user_id,
        j.title::TEXT,
        j.content::JSONB,
        j.post_type::TEXT,
        j.created_at,
        j.privacy::TEXT,
        coalesce(j.views, 0)::bigint AS views,
        j.is_repost,
        j.repost_source_journal_id,
        j.repost_caption::TEXT,
        j.prompt_id,
        u.id AS user_obj_id,
        u.name::TEXT AS user_name,
        u.image_url::TEXT AS user_image_url,
        u.badge::TEXT AS user_badge,
        j.cached_reaction_count::bigint AS like_count,
        j.cached_reaction_count::bigint AS reaction_count,
        j.cached_comment_count::bigint AS comment_count,
        j.cached_bookmark_count::bigint AS bookmark_count,
        (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding))::FLOAT AS semantic_similarity,
        (
            0.80 * (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding))::FLOAT
            + 0.10 * ((COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6 + j.cached_reaction_count * 3 + j.cached_comment_count * 2 + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2)::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            + 0.10 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - j.created_at)) / (90 * 86400.0))
        ) AS composite_score
    FROM public.journals j
    LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
    WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
      AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
      AND j.user_id != p_user_id
      AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
      AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding)) >= 0.30
      AND j.created_at >= now() - interval '90 days'
    ORDER BY (
        0.80 * (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding))::FLOAT
        + 0.10 * ((COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6 + j.cached_reaction_count * 3 + j.cached_comment_count * 2 + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2)::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
        + 0.10 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - j.created_at)) / (90 * 86400.0))
    ) DESC
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$$;

-- 5. RPC: get_interest_posts
-- Finds posts matching a single topic embedding
-- Composite score: 50% semantic + 35% engagement + 15% recency, 30-day default window
DO $$ BEGIN
    DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_interest_posts(vector(384), INT, INT);
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN NULL;
END $$;

    p_topic_embedding vector(384),
    p_limit INT DEFAULT 8,
    p_recency_days INT DEFAULT 30
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    user_id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    views BIGINT,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    username TEXT,
    like_count BIGINT,
    comment_count BIGINT,
    bookmark_count BIGINT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    max_hot INT;
BEGIN
    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL
      AND j2.created_at >= now() - (p_recency_days || ' days')::interval;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH candidates AS (
        SELECT
            j.id,
            j.user_id,
            j.title::TEXT,
            j.post_type::TEXT,
            j.created_at,
            coalesce(j.views, 0)::bigint AS views,
            u.name::TEXT AS user_name,
            u.image_url::TEXT AS user_image_url,
            u.badge::TEXT AS user_badge,
            u.username::TEXT,
            j.cached_reaction_count::bigint AS like_count,
            j.cached_comment_count::bigint AS comment_count,
            j.cached_bookmark_count::bigint AS bookmark_count,
            (1 - (j.embeddings <=> p_topic_embedding))::FLOAT AS sem_sim,
            (
                COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6
                + j.cached_reaction_count * 3
                + j.cached_comment_count * 2
                + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2
            )::INT AS h_score
        FROM public.journals j
        LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
        WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
          AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
          AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
          AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> p_topic_embedding)) >= 0.30
          AND j.created_at >= now() - (p_recency_days || ' days')::interval
        ORDER BY j.embeddings <=> p_topic_embedding
        LIMIT p_limit * 3
    )
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.user_id,
        c.title,
        c.post_type,
        c.created_at,
        c.views,
        c.user_name,
        c.user_image_url,
        c.user_badge,
        c.username,
        c.like_count,
        c.comment_count,
        c.bookmark_count,
        c.sem_sim AS semantic_similarity,
        (
            0.50 * c.sem_sim
            + 0.35 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            + 0.15 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (p_recency_days * 86400.0))
        ) AS composite_score
    FROM candidates c
    ORDER BY (
        0.50 * c.sem_sim
        + 0.35 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
        + 0.15 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (p_recency_days * 86400.0))
    ) DESC
    LIMIT p_limit;
END;
$$;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
-- Personalized Feed: For You + Interest-Based Explore
-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
-- Safe to re-run (uses CREATE OR REPLACE / IF NOT EXISTS)

-- 1. Add interests_embedding column to users
ALTER TABLE public.users
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS interests_embedding vector(384);

-- 2. Create topic_embeddings lookup table (canonical topics from onboarding)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.topic_embeddings (
    topic TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    embedding vector(384) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
);

-- 3. IVFFlat index on users.interests_embedding for fast cosine similarity
-- (lists=10 is fine for < 50k users; bump to sqrt(n) when user base grows)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_interests_embedding
ON public.users
USING ivfflat (interests_embedding vector_cosine_ops)
WITH (lists = 10);

-- 4. RPC: get_for_you_feed
-- Cosine similarity between user's interests_embedding and journal embeddings
-- Composite score: 80% semantic + 10% engagement + 10% recency (90-day window)
-- Excludes own posts & reposts, minimum 0.30 similarity floor, cursor pagination
DO $$ BEGIN
    DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_for_you_feed(UUID, INT, TIMESTAMPTZ);
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN NULL;
END $$;
DO $$ BEGIN
    DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_for_you_feed(UUID, INT, INT);
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN NULL;
END $$;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_for_you_feed(
    p_user_id UUID,
    p_limit INT DEFAULT 10,
    p_offset INT DEFAULT 0
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    user_id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    content JSONB,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    privacy TEXT,
    views BIGINT,
    is_repost BOOLEAN,
    repost_source_journal_id UUID,
    repost_caption TEXT,
    prompt_id INTEGER,
    user_obj_id UUID,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    like_count BIGINT,
    reaction_count BIGINT,
    comment_count BIGINT,
    bookmark_count BIGINT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    user_embedding vector(384);
    max_hot INT;
BEGIN
    -- Get user's interests embedding
    SELECT u.interests_embedding
    INTO user_embedding
    FROM public.users u
    WHERE u.id = p_user_id;

    -- If no embedding, return empty
    IF user_embedding IS NULL THEN
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL
      AND j2.created_at >= now() - interval '90 days';

    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        j.id,
        j.user_id,
        j.title::TEXT,
        j.content::JSONB,
        j.post_type::TEXT,
        j.created_at,
        j.privacy::TEXT,
        coalesce(j.views, 0)::bigint AS views,
        j.is_repost,
        j.repost_source_journal_id,
        j.repost_caption::TEXT,
        j.prompt_id,
        u.id AS user_obj_id,
        u.name::TEXT AS user_name,
        u.image_url::TEXT AS user_image_url,
        u.badge::TEXT AS user_badge,
        j.cached_reaction_count::bigint AS like_count,
        j.cached_reaction_count::bigint AS reaction_count,
        j.cached_comment_count::bigint AS comment_count,
        j.cached_bookmark_count::bigint AS bookmark_count,
        (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding))::FLOAT AS semantic_similarity,
        (
            0.80 * (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding))::FLOAT
            + 0.10 * ((COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6 + j.cached_reaction_count * 3 + j.cached_comment_count * 2 + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2)::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            + 0.10 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - j.created_at)) / (90 * 86400.0))
        ) AS composite_score
    FROM public.journals j
    LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
    WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
      AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
      AND j.user_id != p_user_id
      AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
      AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding)) >= 0.30
      AND j.created_at >= now() - interval '90 days'
    ORDER BY (
        0.80 * (1 - (j.embeddings <=> user_embedding))::FLOAT
        + 0.10 * ((COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6 + j.cached_reaction_count * 3 + j.cached_comment_count * 2 + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2)::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
        + 0.10 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - j.created_at)) / (90 * 86400.0))
    ) DESC
    LIMIT p_limit
    OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$$;

-- 5. RPC: get_interest_posts
-- Finds posts matching a single topic embedding
-- Composite score: 50% semantic + 35% engagement + 15% recency, 30-day default window
DO $$ BEGIN
    DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_interest_posts(vector(384), INT, INT);
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN NULL;
END $$;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_interest_posts(
    p_topic_embedding vector(384),
    p_limit INT DEFAULT 8,
    p_recency_days INT DEFAULT 30
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    user_id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    content JSONB,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    views BIGINT,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    username TEXT,
    like_count BIGINT,
    comment_count BIGINT,
    bookmark_count BIGINT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    max_hot INT;
BEGIN
    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL
      AND j2.created_at >= now() - (p_recency_days || ' days')::interval;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH candidates AS (
        SELECT
            j.id,
            j.user_id,
            j.title::TEXT,
            j.content::JSONB,
            j.post_type::TEXT,
            j.created_at,
            coalesce(j.views, 0)::bigint AS views,
            u.name::TEXT AS user_name,
            u.image_url::TEXT AS user_image_url,
            u.badge::TEXT AS user_badge,
            u.username::TEXT,
            j.cached_reaction_count::bigint AS like_count,
            j.cached_comment_count::bigint AS comment_count,
            j.cached_bookmark_count::bigint AS bookmark_count,
            (1 - (j.embeddings <=> p_topic_embedding))::FLOAT AS sem_sim,
            (
                COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6
                + j.cached_reaction_count * 3
                + j.cached_comment_count * 2
                + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2
            )::INT AS h_score
        FROM public.journals j
        LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
        WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
          AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
          AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
          AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> p_topic_embedding)) >= 0.30
          AND j.created_at >= now() - (p_recency_days || ' days')::interval
        ORDER BY j.embeddings <=> p_topic_embedding
        LIMIT p_limit * 3
    )
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.user_id,
        c.title,
        c.post_type,
        c.created_at,
        c.views,
        c.user_name,
        c.user_image_url,
        c.user_badge,
        c.username,
        c.like_count,
        c.comment_count,
        c.bookmark_count,
        c.sem_sim AS semantic_similarity,
        (
            0.50 * c.sem_sim
            + 0.35 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            + 0.15 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (p_recency_days * 86400.0))
        ) AS composite_score
    FROM candidates c
    ORDER BY (
        0.50 * c.sem_sim
        + 0.35 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
        + 0.15 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (p_recency_days * 86400.0))
    ) DESC
    LIMIT p_limit;
END;
$$;

-- Discovery System: Related Posts Engine
-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
-- Safe to re-run (uses CREATE OR REPLACE)

-- find_related_posts: returns ranked related posts using composite scoring
-- 85% semantic similarity, 10% engagement (hot score), 5% recency
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.find_related_posts;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_related_posts(
    source_post_id UUID,
    match_count INT DEFAULT 8,
    similarity_floor FLOAT DEFAULT 0.40,
    recency_days INT DEFAULT 365
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    user_id UUID,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    username TEXT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    hot_score BIGINT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    source_embedding vector(384);
    source_user_id UUID;
    max_hot BIGINT;
BEGIN
    -- Get the source post's embedding and author
    SELECT j.embeddings, j.user_id
    INTO source_embedding, source_user_id
    FROM public.journals j
    WHERE j.id = source_post_id;

    -- If no embedding, return empty
    IF source_embedding IS NULL THEN
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH candidates AS (
        SELECT
            j.id,
            j.title,
            j.post_type,
            j.created_at,
            j.user_id,
            u.name AS user_name,
            u.image_url AS user_image_url,
            u.badge AS user_badge,
            u.username::TEXT,
            (1 - (j.embeddings <=> source_embedding))::FLOAT AS sem_sim,
            (
                COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6
                + j.cached_reaction_count * 3
                + j.cached_comment_count * 2
                + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2
            )::BIGINT AS h_score
        FROM public.journals j
        LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
        WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
          AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
          AND j.id != source_post_id
          AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
          AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> source_embedding)) >= similarity_floor
        ORDER BY j.embeddings <=> source_embedding
        LIMIT match_count * 4
    )
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.title,
        c.post_type,
        c.created_at,
        c.user_id,
        c.user_name,
        c.user_image_url,
        c.user_badge,
        c.username,
        c.sem_sim AS semantic_similarity,
        c.h_score AS hot_score,
        (
            -- 85% semantic similarity
            0.85 * c.sem_sim
            -- 10% normalized engagement
            + 0.10 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            -- 5% recency (decay over recency_days)
            + 0.05 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (recency_days * 86400.0))
        )
        -- Penalize same-author posts by 15%
        * CASE WHEN c.user_id = source_user_id THEN 0.85 ELSE 1.0 END
        AS composite_score
    FROM candidates c
    ORDER BY composite_score DESC
    LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$;

file.content:
-- Discovery System: Related Posts Engine
-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
-- Safe to re-run (uses CREATE OR REPLACE)

-- find_related_posts: returns ranked related posts using composite scoring
-- 85% semantic similarity, 10% engagement (hot score), 5% recency
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.find_related_posts;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_related_posts(
    source_post_id UUID,
    match_count INT DEFAULT 8,
    similarity_floor FLOAT DEFAULT 0.40,
    recency_days INT DEFAULT 365
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    content JSONB,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    user_id UUID,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    username TEXT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    hot_score BIGINT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    source_embedding vector(384);
    source_user_id UUID;
    max_hot BIGINT;
BEGIN
    -- Get the source post's embedding and author
    SELECT j.embeddings, j.user_id
    INTO source_embedding, source_user_id
    FROM public.journals j
    WHERE j.id = source_post_id;

    -- If no embedding, return empty
    IF source_embedding IS NULL THEN
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH candidates AS (
        SELECT
            j.id,
            j.title,
            j.content::JSONB,
            j.post_type,
            j.created_at,
            j.user_id,
            u.name AS user_name,
            u.image_url AS user_image_url,
            u.badge AS user_badge,
            u.username::TEXT,
            (1 - (j.embeddings <=> source_embedding))::FLOAT AS sem_sim,
            (
                COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6
                + j.cached_reaction_count * 3
                + j.cached_comment_count * 2
                + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2
            )::BIGINT AS h_score
        FROM public.journals j
        LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
        WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
          AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
          AND j.id != source_post_id
          AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
          AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> source_embedding)) >= similarity_floor
        ORDER BY j.embeddings <=> source_embedding
        LIMIT match_count * 4
    )
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.title,
        c.content,
        c.post_type,
        c.created_at,
        c.user_id,
        c.user_name,
        c.user_image_url,
        c.user_badge,
        c.username,
        c.sem_sim AS semantic_similarity,
        c.h_score AS hot_score,
        (
            -- 85% semantic similarity
            0.85 * c.sem_sim
            -- 10% normalized engagement
            + 0.10 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            -- 5% recency (decay over recency_days)
            + 0.05 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (recency_days * 86400.0))
        )
        -- Penalize same-author posts by 15%
        * CASE WHEN c.user_id = source_user_id THEN 0.85 ELSE 1.0 END
        AS composite_score
    FROM candidates c
    ORDER BY composite_score DESC
    LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$;

file.content:
-- Following Feed: Scalable RPC function
-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
-- Safe to re-run (uses CREATE OR REPLACE)
--
-- Replaces the old approach of fetching all following IDs in JS
-- and sending them in a massive IN() clause. Instead, uses a
-- subquery so the IDs never leave the database.

-- Recommended index for this query:
-- CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_journals_following_feed
-- ON journals(user_id, privacy, created_at DESC)
-- WHERE privacy = 'public';

DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_following_feed;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_following_feed(
    p_user_id UUID,
    p_limit INT DEFAULT 5,
    p_before TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NULL
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    user_id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    content JSONB,
    post_type TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    privacy TEXT,
    views INT,
    is_repost BOOLEAN,
    repost_source_journal_id UUID,
    repost_caption TEXT,
    prompt_id INTEGER,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    user_obj_id UUID,
    like_count BIGINT,
    reaction_count BIGINT,
    comment_count BIGINT,
    bookmark_count BIGINT
)
LANGUAGE sql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public
AS $$
    SELECT
        j.id,
        j.user_id,
        j.title,
        j.content::JSONB,
        j.post_type,
        j.created_at,
        j.privacy,
        j.views,
        j.is_repost,
        j.repost_source_journal_id,
        j.repost_caption,
        j.prompt_id,
        u.name AS user_name,
        u.image_url AS user_image_url,
        u.badge AS user_badge,
        u.id AS user_obj_id,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.likes l WHERE l.journal_id = j.id) AS like_count,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.reactions r WHERE r.journal_id = j.id) AS reaction_count,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.comments c WHERE c.post_id = j.id) AS comment_count,
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.bookmarks b WHERE b.journal_id = j.id) AS bookmark_count
    FROM public.journals j
    INNER JOIN public.follows f ON f.following_id = j.user_id AND f.follower_id = p_user_id
    LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
    WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
      AND (p_before IS NULL OR j.created_at < p_before)
    ORDER BY j.created_at DESC, j.id DESC
    LIMIT p_limit;
$$;


CREATE INDEX idx_journals_following_feed
  ON journals(user_id, privacy, created_at DESC)
  WHERE privacy = 'public';

        c.id,
        c.title,
        c.content,
        c.post_type,
        c.created_at,
        c.user_id,
        c.user_name,
        c.user_image_url,
        c.user_badge,
        c.username,
        c.sem_sim AS semantic_similarity,
        c.h_score AS hot_score,
        (
            -- 85% semantic similarity
            0.85 * c.sem_sim
            -- 10% normalized engagement
            + 0.10 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            -- 5% recency (decay over recency_days)
            + 0.05 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (recency_days * 86400.0))
        )
        -- Penalize same-author posts by 15%
        * CASE WHEN c.user_id = source_user_id THEN 0.85 ELSE 1.0 END
        AS composite_score
    FROM candidates c
    ORDER BY composite_score DESC
    LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
-- Discovery System: Related Posts Engine
-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
-- Safe to re-run (uses CREATE OR REPLACE)

-- find_related_posts: returns ranked related posts using composite scoring
-- 85% semantic similarity, 10% engagement (hot score), 5% recency
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.find_related_posts;
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.find_related_posts(
    source_post_id UUID,
    match_count INT DEFAULT 8,
    similarity_floor FLOAT DEFAULT 0.40,
    recency_days INT DEFAULT 365
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    id UUID,
    title TEXT,
    content JSONB,
    post_type TEXT,
    canvas_doc JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    user_id UUID,
    user_name TEXT,
    user_image_url TEXT,
    user_badge TEXT,
    username TEXT,
    semantic_similarity FLOAT,
    hot_score BIGINT,
    composite_score FLOAT
)
LANGUAGE plpgsql
STABLE
SECURITY DEFINER
SET search_path = public, extensions
AS $$
DECLARE
    source_embedding vector(384);
    source_user_id UUID;
    max_hot BIGINT;
BEGIN
    -- Get the source post's embedding and author
    SELECT j.embeddings, j.user_id
    INTO source_embedding, source_user_id
    FROM public.journals j
    WHERE j.id = source_post_id;

    -- If no embedding, return empty
    IF source_embedding IS NULL THEN
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- Get max hot score for normalization
    SELECT COALESCE(MAX(
        COALESCE(j2.views, 0) * 6
        + j2.cached_reaction_count * 3
        + j2.cached_comment_count * 2
        + j2.cached_bookmark_count * 2
    ), 1)
    INTO max_hot
    FROM public.journals j2
    WHERE j2.privacy = 'public'
      AND j2.embeddings IS NOT NULL;

    RETURN QUERY
    WITH candidates AS (
        SELECT
            j.id,
            j.title,
            j.content::JSONB,
            j.post_type,
            j.created_at,
            j.user_id,
            u.name AS user_name,
            u.image_url AS user_image_url,
            u.badge AS user_badge,
            u.username::TEXT,
            (1 - (j.embeddings <=> source_embedding))::FLOAT AS sem_sim,
            (
                COALESCE(j.views, 0) * 6
                + j.cached_reaction_count * 3
                + j.cached_comment_count * 2
                + j.cached_bookmark_count * 2
            )::BIGINT AS h_score
        FROM public.journals j
        LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
        WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
          AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
          AND j.id != source_post_id
          AND COALESCE(j.is_repost, false) = false
          AND (1 - (j.embeddings <=> source_embedding)) >= similarity_floor
        ORDER BY j.embeddings <=> source_embedding
        LIMIT match_count * 4
    )
    SELECT
        c.id,
        c.title,
        c.content,
        c.post_type,
        c.created_at,
        c.user_id,
        c.user_name,
        c.user_image_url,
        c.user_badge,
        c.username,
        c.sem_sim AS semantic_similarity,
        c.h_score AS hot_score,
        (
            -- 85% semantic similarity
            0.85 * c.sem_sim
            -- 10% normalized engagement
            + 0.10 * (c.h_score::FLOAT / GREATEST(max_hot, 1))
            -- 5% recency (decay over recency_days)
            + 0.05 * GREATEST(0, 1.0 - EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - c.created_at)) / (recency_days * 86400.0))
        )
        -- Penalize same-author posts by 15%
        * CASE WHEN c.user_id = source_user_id THEN 0.85 ELSE 1.0 END
        AS composite_score
    FROM candidates c
    ORDER BY composite_score DESC
    LIMIT match_count;
END;
$$;

file.content:
# iskrib_v3 Project Memory

## Architecture
- **Frontend:** React + Vite, BrowserRouter, Lexical rich text editor
- **Backend:** Express.js on port 3000, Supabase (PostgreSQL + Storage)
- **State:** React Query for caching, Context for auth
- **Styling:** CSS variables, glass morphism, Framer Motion animations

## Key Patterns
- Single `<LexicalComposer>` wraps entire app in `App.jsx` (nodes: ImageNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode)
- New independent editors need their own `<LexicalComposer>` instance
- API calls use `fetch` with Bearer token auth, FormData for file uploads
- Backend validates via service layer, controllers are thin wrappers
- Profile customization stored in `profile_layout` JSON column on `users` table
- Image uploads: multer → Sharp (WebP) → Supabase Storage

## File Locations
- Routes: `server/routes/routes.js`
- Sitemap routes: `server/routes/sitemapRoutes.js`
- Upload services: `server/services/uploadService.js`
- URL utils (SITE_URL, makePostUrl): `server/utils/urlUtils.js`
- Client API: `client/API/Api.js`
- SEO config: `client/src/seo/seoConfig.js`
- SEO DOM helpers: `client/src/seo/seoUtils.js`
- SEO manager: `client/src/seo/SeoManager.jsx`
- Post SEO hook: `client/src/seo/usePostSeo.js`
- Profile SEO hook: `client/src/seo/useProfileSeo.js`
- Profile layout: `client/src/utils/profileLayout/`
- Profile page: `client/src/components/ProfilePage/`
- Visited profile: `client/src/components/VisitProfile/`
- Editor: `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/`

## SEO Features (Feb 2026)
- Dynamic sitemaps: `/api/sitemap-index.xml`, `/api/sitemap-posts.xml`, `/api/sitemap-profiles.xml`
- Post pages get Article JSON-LD, proper og:type/title/description/image via `usePostSeo` hook
- Profile pages get Person JSON-LD via `useProfileSeo` hook
- `/@username` routes added alongside legacy `/visitProfile?userId=` (backward compatible)
- `username` column on `users` table (unique, case-insensitive)
- Backfill script: `server/scripts/backfillUsernames.js`
- Username endpoints: `GET /api/user/:username`, `GET /api/check-username/:username`, `POST /api/update-username`
- Signup flow includes username field (auto-suggested from name, real-time availability check)
- `handleClickProfile` accepts optional `clickedUsername` param for `/@username` navigation
- `seoConfig.js` DYNAMIC_PATTERNS support function-based seo (receives pathname)

## Notes Feature (Feb 2026)
- Added customizable notes section to profile pages
- Data in `profile_layout.notes` array, max 10 notes
- Dedicated endpoint `POST /updateProfileLayout` (avoids name/bio validation)
- Each note has its own LexicalComposer instance
- Components: ProfileNotesSection, NoteContainer, NoteStyleEditor, InlineNoteEditor, ReadOnlyNoteView

## Stories Feature (Feb 2026) - Phase 1+2
- Wattpad-style serialized content with chapters + paragraph-level comments
- DB tables: stories, chapters, story_comments, story_votes, reading_progress, story_library
- Migration SQL: `server/sql/stories.sql`
- Storage bucket: `story-covers`
- Backend: `server/services/storyService.js`, `chapterService.js`, `storyInteractService.js`
- Controllers: `server/controller/storyController.js`, `chapterController.js`, `storyInteractController.js`
- Routes: `server/routes/storyRoutes.js` (mounted in server.js via `/api`)
- Client API: `client/API/StoryApi.js`
- Hooks: `client/src/components/Stories/hooks/useStoryData.js`, `useStoryMutations.js`
- Components built (Phase 2): StoryDashboard, StoryEditor, StoryChapterManager, ChapterEditor
- Routes: `/home/stories/dashboard`, `/home/stories/new`, `/:storyId/edit`, `/:storyId/manage`, `/:storyId/chapter/:chapterId/edit`
- Sidebar "Stories" link added to Home.jsx (book icon)
- Future phases: StoryBrowser, StoryDetail, ChapterReader, paragraph comments, profile tabs, SEO

## Discovery Feature (Feb 2026) - Phase 1
- Related posts engine using pgvector cosine similarity + engagement + recency composite scoring
- SQL RPC: `find_related_posts` in `server/sql/discovery.sql`
- Backend: `server/services/discoveryService.js`, `server/controller/discoveryController.js`
- Route: `GET /journal/:journalId/related` (no auth required, public posts only)
- Client API: `getRelatedPosts()` in `client/API/Api.js`
- Component: `client/src/components/Discovery/RelatedPosts.jsx` + CSS
- Integrated into `ContentView.jsx` after `</article>` tag
- Confidence tiers: high (>=0.55, 5 results), medium (>=0.40, 3 results), low (>=0.30, 2 results)
- Dynamic similarity floor: <500 posts=0.25, <2K=0.30, 2K+=0.35
- Never shows <2 results or similarity scores; section hidden when no matches
- Future phases: Similar Thinkers (galaxy_edges), Topic Scaffolding, Writing Prompts

## Onboarding Wizard (Mar 2026)
- 5-step wizard replacing old profile editor modal + WelcomeMessage
- DB columns: `onboarding_completed`, `onboarding_completed_at`, `writing_interests`, `writing_goal` on `users` table
- Migration: `server/sql/onboarding.sql` (backfills existing users as onboarded)
- Backend: `completeOnboardingService` in `uploadService.js`, `completeOnboardingController` in `uploadController.js`
- Route: `POST /api/complete-onboarding` (requireAuth)
- `checkUserService` returns `onboarding_completed`; controller returns `onboardingCompleted` field
- Client API: `completeOnboarding()` in `Api.js`
- Components: `client/src/components/Onboarding/` — OnboardingWizard, steps/ (Welcome, ProfileSetup, Interests, FeatureShowcase, FirstAction), components/ (StepProgressBar, TypewriterText)
- CSS: `OnboardingWizard.css` — warm editorial luxury aesthetic (Playfair Display, Lora, amber accents, glass morphism)

# iskrib_v3 Project Memory

## Architecture
- **Frontend:** React + Vite, BrowserRouter, Lexical rich text editor
- **Backend:** Express.js on port 3000, Supabase (PostgreSQL + Storage)
- **State:** React Query for caching, Context for auth
- **Styling:** CSS variables, glass morphism, Framer Motion animations

## Key Patterns
- Single `<LexicalComposer>` wraps entire app in `App.jsx` (nodes: ImageNode, HeadingNode, QuoteNode)
- New independent editors need their own `<LexicalComposer>` instance
- API calls use `fetch` with Bearer token auth, FormData for file uploads
- Backend validates via service layer, controllers are thin wrappers
- Profile customization stored in `profile_layout` JSON column on `users` table
- Image uploads: multer → Sharp (WebP) → Supabase Storage

## File Locations
- Routes: `server/routes/routes.js`
- Sitemap routes: `server/routes/sitemapRoutes.js`
- Upload services: `server/services/uploadService.js`
- URL utils (SITE_URL, makePostUrl): `server/utils/urlUtils.js`
- Client API: `client/API/Api.js`
- SEO config: `client/src/seo/seoConfig.js`
- SEO DOM helpers: `client/src/seo/seoUtils.js`
- SEO manager: `client/src/seo/SeoManager.jsx`
- Post SEO hook: `client/src/seo/usePostSeo.js`
- Profile SEO hook: `client/src/seo/useProfileSeo.js`
- Profile layout: `client/src/utils/profileLayout/`
- Profile page: `client/src/components/ProfilePage/`
- Visited profile: `client/src/components/VisitProfile/`
- Editor: `client/src/components/HomePage/Editor/`

## SEO Features (Feb 2026)
- Dynamic sitemaps: `/api/sitemap-index.xml`, `/api/sitemap-posts.xml`, `/api/sitemap-profiles.xml`
- Post pages get Article JSON-LD, proper og:type/title/description/image via `usePostSeo` hook
- Profile pages get Person JSON-LD via `useProfileSeo` hook
- `/@username` routes added alongside legacy `/visitProfile?userId=` (backward compatible)
- `username` column on `users` table (unique, case-insensitive)
- Backfill script: `server/scripts/backfillUsernames.js`
- Username endpoints: `GET /api/user/:username`, `GET /api/check-username/:username`, `POST /api/update-username`
- Signup flow includes username field (auto-suggested from name, real-time availability check)
- `handleClickProfile` accepts optional `clickedUsername` param for `/@username` navigation
- `seoConfig.js` DYNAMIC_PATTERNS support function-based seo (receives pathname)

## Notes Feature (Feb 2026)
- Added customizable notes section to profile pages
- Data in `profile_layout.notes` array, max 10 notes
- Dedicated endpoint `POST /updateProfileLayout` (avoids name/bio validation)
- Each note has its own LexicalComposer instance
- Components: ProfileNotesSection, NoteContainer, NoteStyleEditor, InlineNoteEditor, ReadOnlyNoteView

## Stories Feature (Feb 2026) - Phase 1+2
- Wattpad-style serialized content with chapters + paragraph-level comments
- DB tables: stories, chapters, story_comments, story_votes, reading_progress, story_library
- Migration SQL: `server/sql/stories.sql`
- Storage bucket: `story-covers`
- Backend: `server/services/storyService.js`, `chapterService.js`, `storyInteractService.js`
- Controllers: `server/controller/storyController.js`, `chapterController.js`, `storyInteractController.js`
- Routes: `server/routes/storyRoutes.js` (mounted in server.js via `/api`)
- Client API: `client/API/StoryApi.js`
- Hooks: `client/src/components/Stories/hooks/useStoryData.js`, `useStoryMutations.js`
- Components built (Phase 2): StoryDashboard, StoryEditor, StoryChapterManager, ChapterEditor
- Routes: `/home/stories/dashboard`, `/home/stories/new`, `/:storyId/edit`, `/:storyId/manage`, `/:storyId/chapter/:chapterId/edit`
- Sidebar "Stories" link added to Home.jsx (book icon)
- Future phases: StoryBrowser, StoryDetail, ChapterReader, paragraph comments, profile tabs, SEO

## Discovery Feature (Feb 2026) - Phase 1
- Related posts engine using pgvector cosine similarity + engagement + recency composite scoring
- SQL RPC: `find_related_posts` in `server/sql/discovery.sql`
- Backend: `server/services/discoveryService.js`, `server/controller/discoveryController.js`
- Route: `GET /journal/:journalId/related` (no auth required, public posts only)
- Client API: `getRelatedPosts()` in `client/API/Api.js`
- Component: `client/src/components/Discovery/RelatedPosts.jsx` + CSS
- Integrated into `ContentView.jsx` after `</article>` tag
- Confidence tiers: high (>=0.55, 5 results), medium (>=0.40, 3 results), low (>=0.30, 2 results)
- Dynamic similarity floor: <500 posts=0.25, <2K=0.30, 2K+=0.35
- Never shows <2 results or similarity scores; section hidden when no matches
- Future phases: Similar Thinkers (galaxy_edges), Topic Scaffolding, Writing Prompts

## Onboarding Wizard (Mar 2026)
- 5-step wizard replacing old profile editor modal + WelcomeMessage
- DB columns: `onboarding_completed`, `onboarding_completed_at`, `writing_interests`, `writing_goal` on `users` table
- Migration: `server/sql/onboarding.sql` (backfills existing users as onboarded)
- Backend: `completeOnboardingService` in `uploadService.js`, `completeOnboardingController` in `uploadController.js`
- Route: `POST /api/complete-onboarding` (requireAuth)
- `checkUserService` returns `onboarding_completed`; controller returns `onboardingCompleted` field
- Client API: `completeOnboarding()` in `Api.js`
- Components: `client/src/components/Onboarding/` — OnboardingWizard, steps/ (Welcome, ProfileSetup, Interests, FeatureShowcase, FirstAction), components/ (StepProgressBar, TypewriterText)
- CSS: `OnboardingWizard.css` — warm editorial luxury aesthetic (Playfair Display, Lora, amber accents, glass morphism)
- Home.jsx: `showOnboarding` state, `checkOnboarding` useEffect, `handleOnboardingComplete` callback
- `userExists` prop skips profile step (starts at step 2) for interrupted flows
- Final step CTA adapts to `writingGoal`: journal/publish→write, stories→story, explore→feed

## Mobile App UI Parity (Mar 2026)
- **Location:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_mobile_app\apps\mobile\`
- **React Native 0.84.0** with React 19, monorepo under `apps/mobile/`
- **Theme system:** `src/theme/tokens.ts` (light/dark palettes), `ThemeProvider.tsx`, `useThemeStyles.ts`
- **Old `colors.ts` deleted** — all 20+ screens migrated to `useTheme()` hook
- **Typography:** 4 font families (Outfit, Lora, Lexend Deca, Playfair Display) in `assets/fonts/`, linked via `react-native.config.js`
- **Type scale:** `src/theme/typography.ts` — hero, h1-h3, body, ui, caption, button presets
- **Spacing/shadows:** `src/theme/spacing.ts` — spacing tokens, radii, platform-aware shadows
- **Icons:** `src/components/icons/Icon.tsx` — 20 SVG icons via react-native-svg (Heart, Comment, Bookmark, etc.)
- **Core components:** Avatar, PostCard+ActionBar, SearchInput, Chip, GlassCard, SkeletonLoader, EmptyState, Toast
- **LexicalRenderer:** `src/lib/content/LexicalRenderer.tsx` — walks Lexical JSON tree, renders native Text/View/Image
- **Auth screens:** Branded ISKRIB header, gold focus rings, username field on signup, ForgotPasswordScreen
- **Home feed:** PostCard integration, branded header, filter Chips, FAB write button
- **Profile:** Glass card stats, badge pills, colored header bg, avatar with badge ring
- **Notifications:** Avatar + icon overlay, unread gold dot, tap-to-navigate
- **Explore:** SearchInput, tab filters (All/Users/Posts), avatar user rows
- **RelatedPosts:** Horizontal scroll cards below post content
- **Tab bar:** Icons (Home/Compass/Book/Bell/User) with amber active tint
- **Nav types:** Extended with VisitProfile, EditProfile, Settings, FollowList
- **Completed phases:** 0 (Foundation), 1 (Auth), 2 (Feed), 3 (Content), 4A (Profile), 6 (Discovery/Notifs/Explore), 9C (Tab Icons)
- **Remaining phases:** 4B-D (VisitProfile, EditProfile, image picker), 5 (Social), 7 (Stories polish), 8 (Animations), 9A-B (Settings), 10 (Missing features)

content.text:
Here's a summary of all the changes made:

## Egress Audit — Implemented Fixes

### Fix 1: QueryClient defaults (`client/src/main.jsx`)
- Added `staleTime: 5min` and `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` as global defaults
- Every `useQuery` that doesn't set its own staleTime now benefits automatically

### Fix 3: Removed broad `onSettled` invalidations (`client/src/utils/useMutation.js`)
- Removed `invalidateQueries(['journals'])` from bookmark, like, and reaction mutations
- Removed `invalidateQueries(['journals'])` from repost's `onSuccess` (kept `userJournals` since reposts create new posts)
- Optimistic updates already handle the UI; `onError` rolls back on failure

### Fix 4: Replaced `SELECT *` on users (`server/services/getUserDataService.js`)
- Added `USER_PROFILE_SELECT` constant with 15 explicit columns (excludes `interests_embedding` — 3KB vector)
- Applied to both `getUserByUsernameService` and `getUserDataService`
- Changed follow count queries from `select('*')` to `select('id')` for head-only counts

### Fix 5: Added image resize (`server/routes/routes.js`)
- Added `BUCKET_RESIZE_LIMITS` map with per-bucket size constraints
- Avatars: 400x400 cover, Backgrounds: 1920x1080, Story covers: 800x1200, Journal images: 1600x1600
- Uses `withoutEnlargement: true` to avoid upscaling small images

### Fix 6: Share image LRU cache (`server/server.js`)
- Added in-memory Map-based LRU cache (200 entries, 1hr TTL)
- Both `/share/post/:id/image` and `/share/u/:username/image` check cache before hitting Supabase Storage
- Cache bypass for debug mode

### Fix 8: checkUser → use existing auth userData (`client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx`)
- Removed separate `checkUser()` API call on every app load
- Now reads `onboarding_completed` from the auth context's `user.userData[0]` (already fetched)

### Fix 9: Story comments LIMIT (`server/services/storyInteractService.js`)
- Added `.limit(50)` to top-level comments query
- Added `.limit(200)` to replies query

### Fix 10: getDynamicFloor cache (`server/services/discoveryService.js`)
- Added 5-minute in-memory TTL cache for the `COUNT(*)` query
- Returns cached value if DB query fails

### Fix 11: searchFollowingUsers bounded (`server/services/getService.js`)
- Added `.limit(2000)` to follows fetch to prevent unbounded row transfer

### Fix 12: promptService unique count (`server/services/promptService.js`)
- Replaced full `user_id` row fetch + JS Set dedup with `select('id', { count: 'exact', head: true })`

### Fix 13: Explore interests staleTime (`client/src/components/HomePage/explore/ExplorePage.jsx`)
- Increased from 10min to 30min for the 8-parallel-pgvector-scan query
- Changed query key from `access_token` to `user.id`

### Fix 14: Query key token rotation fixes (multiple files)
- Changed all `queryKey: [..., session?.access_token]` to `session?.user?.id`
- Affected: DashboardBriefing, WeeklyRecapCard, PostCards (following + for-you feeds), notifications, unread notifications, MyOpinions

### Low priority: `SELECT *` → `select('id')` for count queries (`server/routes/routes.js`)
- Changed 3 follow count queries from `select('*', {count, head})` to `select('id', {count, head})`

---

### Deferred: Content removal from feeds (Fix 2)
- **Not implemented** — the client's `ParseContent()` extracts preview text and thumbnails from the Lexical JSON `content` field in all feed components
- **Future work needed:** Add server-side `preview_text` + `thumbnail_url` fields, migrate all feed components to use them, then remove `content` from feed queries
- `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` and `JOURNAL_METADATA_WITH_COUNTS_SELECT` constants are defined and ready to use

### Not code changes: CDN setup (Fix 7)
- Infrastructure task — put Cloudflare in front of Supabase Storage via custom subdomain

content.content: 1→# Supabase Egress Audit — Findings & Fix Plan 2→ 3→## Context 4→Full codebase audit for patterns that will blow Supabase egress (database transfer + storage bandwidth). Three areas analyzed: DB query payloads, storage/image serving, and client-side refetch behavior. 5→ 6→**Verdict: Yes, this codebase has several patterns that will cause excessive egress at scale.** 7→ 8→--- 9→ 10→## CRITICAL — Fix These First 11→ 12→### 1. `content` (Lexical JSON) returned in ALL feed/list queries 13→Every feed endpoint returns full post content (5-100KB per post) even though list views only show previews. A `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` constant exists but is **never used**. 14→ 15→| Affected | File | Lines | 16→|----------|------|-------| 17→| Public feed | `server/services/getService.js` | L441 | 18→| User journals | `server/services/getService.js` | L821 | 19→| Visited profile | `server/services/getService.js` | L917 | 20→| Bookmarks | `server/services/getService.js` | L1117-1129 | 21→| Repost sources | `server/services/getService.js` | L95-99 | 22→| Following feed RPC | `server/sql/following_feed.sql` | L52 | 23→| For-you feed RPC | `server/sql/personalized_feed.sql` | L102 | 24→| Related posts RPC | `server/sql/discovery.sql` | L68 | 25→| Explore interests | `server/services/exploreInterestsService.js` | L38-83 (8 parallel pgvector scans, up to 64 posts with full content) | 26→ 27→**Fix:** Use `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` (already defined at L35-48) in all list endpoints. Remove `content` from all SQL RPC return signatures. Only return `content` in `getJournalByIdService` (single post detail). 28→ 29→**Estimated savings:** 60-80% of database egress. 30→ 31→--- 32→ 33→### 2. No CDN in front of Supabase Storage 34→All 5 storage buckets (`avatars`, `background`, `journal-images`, `story-covers`, media gallery) serve images directly from `https://.supabase.co/storage/...`. No CDN configured anywhere. Every image request = full Supabase storage egress. 35→ 36→Avatar URLs are embedded in every feed response, comment, notification, and repost — meaning 5-20 Supabase Storage hits per feed page load across all users. 37→ 38→**Fix:** Put Cloudflare (free tier) or another CDN in front of Supabase Storage via a custom subdomain (e.g., `media.iskrib.com`). 39→ 40→**Estimated savings:** 70-90% of storage egress once cache warms. 41→ 42→--- 43→ 44→### 3. `QueryClient` has zero default configuration 45→`client/src/main.jsx` L9: `new QueryClient()` with no defaults means: 46→- `staleTime: 0` — every query is immediately stale 47→- `refetchOnWindowFocus: true` — every tab switch refetches all active queries 48→- Any `useQuery` that forgets to set its own `staleTime` silently refetches on every render 49→ 50→**Fix:** 51→```js 52→const queryClient = new QueryClient({ 53→ defaultOptions: { 54→ queries: { 55→ staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5, // 5 min default 56→ refetchOnWindowFocus: false, 57→ } 58→ } 59→}); 60→``` 61→ 62→--- 63→ 64→### 4. Broad `invalidateQueries` on every social action 65→`client/src/utils/useMutation.js` L95-97, L195-197, L455-459: Every like, bookmark, reaction, repost, and comment invalidates `['journals']` which matches ALL feed queries. If a user has scrolled 5 pages deep, this triggers 5 sequential refetches per feed variant — potentially 15+ API calls from a single like. 66→ 67→**Fix:** Use optimistic updates only (already partially implemented) and either remove `onSettled` invalidation or scope it to the specific active feed query key. 68→ 69→--- 70→ 71→## HIGH — Fix Next 72→ 73→### 5. `SELECT *` on `users` table includes 384-float vector 74→`server/services/getUserDataService.js` L10, L61: `SELECT *` returns `interests_embedding` (~3KB vector), `profile_layout` JSON, and all other wide columns on every profile load and auth check. 75→ 76→**Fix:** Replace with explicit column list excluding `interests_embedding` and other server-only fields. 77→ 78→### 6. Double egress on share image routes 79→`server/server.js` L421-488, L621-737, L801-883: Share image routes fetch images FROM Supabase Storage through Express, process them, then serve to crawlers. No caching — every crawler hit = fresh Supabase fetch. 80→ 81→**Fix:** Add in-memory LRU cache (e.g., `lru-cache` with 1-hour TTL) for generated share images. 82→ 83→### 7. Avatar/cover images stored at full resolution 84→`server/routes/routes.js` L180-184: `imageUploader` for avatars and backgrounds applies `.webp({quality: 80})` but **no `.resize()`**. A 6000x4000 camera photo is stored and served at full resolution. 85→ 86→**Fix:** Add `.resize(400, 400, { fit: 'inside' })` for avatars, `.resize(1920, 1080, { fit: 'inside' })` for backgrounds, `.resize(800, 1200, { fit: 'inside' })` for story covers. 87→ 88→--- 89→ 90→## MEDIUM — Should Fix 91→ 92→### 8. `checkUser` is a raw uncached fetch on every app load 93→`client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` L228-242: `checkUser()` called in `useEffect` with no React Query caching. Once onboarding is complete, this check is useless but still fires every session. 94→ 95→**Fix:** Wrap in `useQuery` with `staleTime: Infinity` after onboarding completes, or use the existing `userData` query to check `onboarding_completed`. 96→ 97→### 9. Story chapter comments have no LIMIT 98→`server/services/storyInteractService.js` L205-244: All comments + all replies fetched unbounded. A popular chapter with 500+ comments transfers everything at once. 99→ 100→**Fix:** Add pagination (LIMIT 50 with cursor). 101→ 102→### 10. `getDynamicFloor()` COUNT query on every post view 103→`server/services/discoveryService.js` L9-23: Runs `SELECT count(*)` on all public journals every time someone views a post's related posts section. 104→ 105→**Fix:** Cache in memory with 5-minute TTL. 106→ 107→### 11. `searchFollowingUsersService` fetches unbounded follows 108→`server/services/getService.js` L1265-1268: Fetches ALL follow rows to Node.js, then sends as IN() clause. User following 2000 people = 2000 rows transferred. 109→ 110→**Fix:** Replace with server-side RPC join. 111→ 112→### 12. `promptService` unique count fetches all rows 113→`server/services/promptService.js` L82-88: Fetches all `user_id` values to count uniques in JS instead of `COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)` in SQL. 114→ 115→### 13. Explore interests staleTime too short for 8 parallel pgvector scans 116→`client/src/components/HomePage/explore/ExplorePage.jsx`: 10-minute staleTime for a query that fires 8 concurrent pgvector scans returning 64 posts. 117→ 118→**Fix:** Increase to 30+ minutes. 119→ 120→### 14. `DashboardBriefing` weekly recap keyed on `access_token` 121→`client/src/components/DashboardBriefing/DashboardBriefing.jsx` L39: Query key includes `session?.access_token` — when token rotates, cache key changes and data refetches. 122→ 123→**Fix:** Key on `session?.user?.id` instead. 124→ 125→--- 126→ 127→## LOW — Nice to Have 128→ 129→- `SELECT *` on bookmarks/follows for existence checks → use `.select('id')` only 130→- Missing `loading="lazy"` on many avatar `` tags (Sidebar, ProfileEditModal, Visitprofile, StoryDetail) 131→- `Notifications.jsx` scroll `useEffect` missing dependency array (re-registers listener every render) 132→- API `Cache-Control` only 10s for public feeds (`server.js` L89) 133→ 134→--- 135→ 136→## Implementation Order 137→ 138→1. **QueryClient defaults** (main.jsx) — 2 lines, immediate global impact 139→2. **Remove `content` from list queries** — swap to `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` in getService.js + update SQL RPCs 140→3. **Scope mutation invalidations** in useMutation.js 141→4. **Replace `SELECT *` on users** with explicit columns in getUserDataService.js 142→5. **Add image resize** to imageUploader in routes.js 143→6. **Add share image caching** in server.js 144→7. **CDN setup** (Cloudflare) — infrastructure, not code 145→8. Remaining medium/low items 146→ 147→## Verification 148→- After fixes 1-4: Monitor Supabase dashboard egress metrics over 24h; expect 50-70% reduction in database egress 149→- After fix 5: Check average image size in storage buckets drops 150→- After fix 7: Storage egress should drop 70-90% 151→- Run the app through all flows (feed scroll, post view, like/bookmark, profile visit, search, explore) and check Network tab for payload sizes and request counts 152→

A `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` constant exists but is **never used**. | Affected | File | Lines | |----------|------|-------| | Public feed | `server/services/getService.js` | L441 | | User journals | `server/services/getService.js` | L821 | | Visited profile | `server/services/getService.js` | L917 | | Bookmarks | `server/services/getService.js` | L1117-1129 | | Repost sources | `server/services/getService.js` | L95-99 | | Following feed RPC | `server/sql/following_feed.sql` | L52 | | For-you feed RPC | `server/sql/personalized_feed.sql` | L102 | | Related posts RPC | `server/sql/discovery.sql` | L68 | | Explore interests | `server/services/exploreInterestsService.js` | L38-83 (8 parallel pgvector scans, up to 64 posts with full content) | **Fix:** Use `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` (already defined at L35-48) in all list endpoints. Remove `content` from all SQL RPC return signatures. Only return `content` in `getJournalByIdService` (single post detail). **Estimated savings:** 60-80% of database egress. --- ### 2. No CDN in front of Supabase Storage All 5 storage buckets (`avatars`, `background`, `journal-images`, `story-covers`, media gallery) serve images directly from `https://.supabase.co/storage/...`. No CDN configured anywhere. Every image request = full Supabase storage egress. Avatar URLs are embedded in every feed response, comment, notification, and repost — meaning 5-20 Supabase Storage hits per feed page load across all users. **Fix:** Put Cloudflare (free tier) or another CDN in front of Supabase Storage via a custom subdomain (e.g., `media.iskrib.com`). **Estimated savings:** 70-90% of storage egress once cache warms. --- ### 3. `QueryClient` has zero default configuration `client/src/main.jsx` L9: `new QueryClient()` with no defaults means: - `staleTime: 0` — every query is immediately stale - `refetchOnWindowFocus: true` — every tab switch refetches all active queries - Any `useQuery` that forgets to set its own `staleTime` silently refetches on every render **Fix:** ```js const queryClient = new QueryClient({ defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 5, // 5 min default refetchOnWindowFocus: false, } } }); ``` --- ### 4. Broad `invalidateQueries` on every social action `client/src/utils/useMutation.js` L95-97, L195-197, L455-459: Every like, bookmark, reaction, repost, and comment invalidates `['journals']` which matches ALL feed queries. If a user has scrolled 5 pages deep, this triggers 5 sequential refetches per feed variant — potentially 15+ API calls from a single like. **Fix:** Use optimistic updates only (already partially implemented) and either remove `onSettled` invalidation or scope it to the specific active feed query key. --- ## HIGH — Fix Next ### 5. `SELECT *` on `users` table includes 384-float vector `server/services/getUserDataService.js` L10, L61: `SELECT *` returns `interests_embedding` (~3KB vector), `profile_layout` JSON, and all other wide columns on every profile load and auth check. **Fix:** Replace with explicit column list excluding `interests_embedding` and other server-only fields. ### 6. Double egress on share image routes `server/server.js` L421-488, L621-737, L801-883: Share image routes fetch images FROM Supabase Storage through Express, process them, then serve to crawlers. No caching — every crawler hit = fresh Supabase fetch. **Fix:** Add in-memory LRU cache (e.g., `lru-cache` with 1-hour TTL) for generated share images. ### 7. Avatar/cover images stored at full resolution `server/routes/routes.js` L180-184: `imageUploader` for avatars and backgrounds applies `.webp({quality: 80})` but **no `.resize()`**. A 6000x4000 camera photo is stored and served at full resolution. **Fix:** Add `.resize(400, 400, { fit: 'inside' })` for avatars, `.resize(1920, 1080, { fit: 'inside' })` for backgrounds, `.resize(800, 1200, { fit: 'inside' })` for story covers. --- ## MEDIUM — Should Fix ### 8. `checkUser` is a raw uncached fetch on every app load `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` L228-242: `checkUser()` called in `useEffect` with no React Query caching. Once onboarding is complete, this check is useless but still fires every session. **Fix:** Wrap in `useQuery` with `staleTime: Infinity` after onboarding completes, or use the existing `userData` query to check `onboarding_completed`. ### 9. Story chapter comments have no LIMIT `server/services/storyInteractService.js` L205-244: All comments + all replies fetched unbounded. A popular chapter with 500+ comments transfers everything at once. **Fix:** Add pagination (LIMIT 50 with cursor). ### 10. `getDynamicFloor()` COUNT query on every post view `server/services/discoveryService.js` L9-23: Runs `SELECT count(*)` on all public journals every time someone views a post's related posts section. **Fix:** Cache in memory with 5-minute TTL. ### 11. `searchFollowingUsersService` fetches unbounded follows `server/services/getService.js` L1265-1268: Fetches ALL follow rows to Node.js, then sends as IN() clause. User following 2000 people = 2000 rows transferred. **Fix:** Replace with server-side RPC join. ### 12. `promptService` unique count fetches all rows `server/services/promptService.js` L82-88: Fetches all `user_id` values to count uniques in JS instead of `COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)` in SQL. ### 13. Explore interests staleTime too short for 8 parallel pgvector scans `client/src/components/HomePage/explore/ExplorePage.jsx`: 10-minute staleTime for a query that fires 8 concurrent pgvector scans returning 64 posts. **Fix:** Increase to 30+ minutes. ### 14. `DashboardBriefing` weekly recap keyed on `access_token` `client/src/components/DashboardBriefing/DashboardBriefing.jsx` L39: Query key includes `session?.access_token` — when token rotates, cache key changes and data refetches. **Fix:** Key on `session?.user?.id` instead. --- ## LOW — Nice to Have - `SELECT *` on bookmarks/follows for existence checks → use `.select('id')` only - Missing `loading="lazy"` on many avatar `` tags (Sidebar, ProfileEditModal, Visitprofile, StoryDetail) - `Notifications.jsx` scroll `useEffect` missing dependency array (re-registers listener every render) - API `Cache-Control` only 10s for public feeds (`server.js` L89) --- ## Implementation Order 1. **QueryClient defaults** (main.jsx) — 2 lines, immediate global impact 2. **Remove `content` from list queries** — swap to `JOURNAL_METADATA_SELECT` in getService.js + update SQL RPCs 3. **Scope mutation invalidations** in useMutation.js 4. **Replace `SELECT *` on users** with explicit columns in getUserDataService.js 5. **Add image resize** to imageUploader in routes.js 6. **Add share image caching** in server.js 7. **CDN setup** (Cloudflare) — infrastructure, not code 8. Remaining medium/low items ## Verification - After fixes 1-4: Monitor Supabase dashboard egress metrics over 24h; expect 50-70% reduction in database egress - After fix 5: Check average image size in storage buckets drops - After fix 7: Storage egress should drop 70-90% - Run the app through all flows (feed scroll, post view, like/bookmark, profile visit, search, explore) and check Network tab for payload sizes and request counts

content.text: I now have everything needed. Let me compile the full assessment. --- ## Assessment: Current State After Egress Optimization Work --- ### 1. How PostCards.jsx uses `ParseContent(journal.content)` File: `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` At render time, inside the per-card render loop (line 689-692): ```js const parsedContent = !isRepost ? ParseContent(journal.content) : null; const wholeText = parsedContent?.wholeText || ''; const previewText = parsedContent?.slicedText || ''; const thumbnail = parsedContent?.firstImage?.src; ``` All three derived values are consumed: - **`thumbnail`** (`firstImage.src`) — rendered as an `` card thumbnail if present (line 774-781). - **`previewText`** (`slicedText`, 215-char truncation) — rendered as the post excerpt text body. - **`wholeText`** — passed to `CalculateText()` for reading-time display (line 869), and forwarded to `viewContent()` / `clickContent()` so the content viewer slide-in panel has the full plain-text string available without reparsing. For repost cards, a second parse is done (line 698-699): ```js const repostSourceParsed = repostSource ? ParseContent(repostSource.content) : null; const repostSourcePreviewText = repostSourceParsed?.slicedText || ''; ``` So: **every feed card parse extracts preview text, the first image URL, and the full plain text** — all three — on the client, on every render. --- ### 2. `ParseContent` — Definition and Return Shape Both copies are identical: - **Client:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/parseData.js` - **Server:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/server/utils/parseData.js` **What it takes:** A raw Lexical JSON string (the `content` column from the DB). **Algorithm:** 1. `JSON.parse(contentString)` to get the Lexical AST. 2. Recursively walks `root.children`, handling nodes of type `paragraph`, `heading`, and top-level `image`. 3. Collects text from `text`-type child nodes; collects image metadata from `image`-type child nodes. **Return shape:** ```js { text: string[], // array of per-paragraph/heading text strings wholeText: string, // all text joined with spaces (full plain text) slicedText: string, // wholeText truncated at 215 chars with '...' suffix images: Array<{src, width, height}>, // ALL images found in document firstImage: {src, width, height} | null // first image found, or null } ``` Key notes: - `slicedText` is always `<= 215` chars (plus `...`), never longer. - `firstImage` is used everywhere as the card thumbnail — it is just `images[0]`. - On any parse error or empty/non-string input, returns the same shape with empty/null defaults. - The server copy is strictly a utility — it is not used in any endpoint response; it's only called inside `uploadService.js` to extract `wholeText` for embedding generation (see item 3). --- ### 3. `uploadService.js` — What Happens at Write Time File: `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/server/services/uploadService.js` There are two write paths: **create** (`uploadJournalContentService`, line 223) and **update** (`updateJournalService`, line 368). Both paths do the following identically: ```js const parseData = parseTextContentSafely(resolvedContent); // calls server ParseContent const embeddingBody = parseData.wholeText || ''; const embeddings = await GenerateEmbeddings(resolvedTitle, embeddingBody); ``` Then insert/update into `journals` with this payload: ```js { user_id, title, post_type, content, // raw Lexical JSON stored as-is embeddings // pgvector embedding } ``` **What is NOT stored at write time:** `preview_text`, `thumbnail_url`, `slicedText`, or `firstImage.src`. The server already runs `ParseContent` at write time (to feed the embedding), but **it discards everything except `wholeText`**. The thumbnail and preview text are thrown away and re-derived on the client on every render. This means there is a clear, low-risk hook already in place: at line 261 and line 418, `parseData.firstImage?.src` and `parseData.slicedText` are already available — they just are not being persisted. --- ### 4. Journals Table Schema — No Preview/Thumbnail Columns None of the SQL files contain a `CREATE TABLE journals` statement (the table predates all migration files in `server/sql/`). Searching all SQL files for `preview_text`, `thumbnail_url`, `cover_image`, or `preview_image` returns **zero matches**. The known columns added via migrations are: | Source file | Columns added | |---|---| | `scalability_fixes.sql` | `cached_reaction_count`, `cached_comment_count`, `cached_bookmark_count` | | `scalability_fixes.sql` | `content jsonb` (referenced in function), `repost_source_journal_id uuid` | | `hottest_tracker.sql` | indexes only | | `semantic_search.sql` | `embeddings vector(...)` | **No `preview_text` or `thumbnail_url` column exists.** This is the gap the egress optimization would need to fill. --- ### 5. All Components That Use `ParseContent` (9 call-sites across 8 files) | File | Fields Used | |---|---| | `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/ProfilePostCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` (used twice: list view + grid view) | | `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/VisitedProfilePostCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` (used twice: list view + grid view) | | `client/src/components/Bookmarks/Bookmarks.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/HomePage/explore/ExplorePage.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` (called twice: hero card + runner cards) | | `client/src/components/HomePage/explore/InterestSections.jsx` | `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/Notifications/UnreadNotificationCard.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | **Total: 8 consuming components** (plus `parseData.js` itself = 9 files in the grep results). Every single consumer uses all three of the expensive derived values: thumbnail, preview text, and whole text. --- ### Summary for Egress Optimization The situation is very clean: 1. The `content` column (full Lexical JSON, potentially many KB) is fetched from the DB and shipped to every client for every card in every feed — solely so the client can call `ParseContent()` and extract three small strings. 2. The server already calls `ParseContent` at write time (lines 255-261 and 412-418) — it just discards `slicedText` and `firstImage.src` after using `wholeText` for embeddings. 3. No `preview_text` or `thumbnail_url` columns exist yet on the `journals` table. 4. All 8 consuming components use the same three fields (`slicedText`, `firstImage.src`, `wholeText`). Adding two DB columns (`preview_text varchar(215)` and `thumbnail_url text`) and populating them at write time would allow the feed queries to stop selecting `content` entirely for list views — eliminating the dominant source of egress.

content.text: I now have everything needed. Let me compile the full assessment. --- ## Assessment: Current State After Egress Optimization Work --- ### 1. How PostCards.jsx uses `ParseContent(journal.content)` File: `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` At render time, inside the per-card render loop (line 689-692): ```js const parsedContent = !isRepost ? ParseContent(journal.content) : null; const wholeText = parsedContent?.wholeText || ''; const previewText = parsedContent?.slicedText || ''; const thumbnail = parsedContent?.firstImage?.src; ``` All three derived values are consumed: - **`thumbnail`** (`firstImage.src`) — rendered as an `` card thumbnail if present (line 774-781). - **`previewText`** (`slicedText`, 215-char truncation) — rendered as the post excerpt text body. - **`wholeText`** — passed to `CalculateText()` for reading-time display (line 869), and forwarded to `viewContent()` / `clickContent()` so the content viewer slide-in panel has the full plain-text string available without reparsing. For repost cards, a second parse is done (line 698-699): ```js const repostSourceParsed = repostSource ? ParseContent(repostSource.content) : null; const repostSourcePreviewText = repostSourceParsed?.slicedText || ''; ``` So: **every feed card parse extracts preview text, the first image URL, and the full plain text** — all three — on the client, on every render. --- ### 2. `ParseContent` — Definition and Return Shape Both copies are identical: - **Client:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/parseData.js` - **Server:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/server/utils/parseData.js` **What it takes:** A raw Lexical JSON string (the `content` column from the DB). **Algorithm:** 1. `JSON.parse(contentString)` to get the Lexical AST. 2. Recursively walks `root.children`, handling nodes of type `paragraph`, `heading`, and top-level `image`. 3. Collects text from `text`-type child nodes; collects image metadata from `image`-type child nodes. **Return shape:** ```js { text: string[], // array of per-paragraph/heading text strings wholeText: string, // all text joined with spaces (full plain text) slicedText: string, // wholeText truncated at 215 chars with '...' suffix images: Array<{src, width, height}>, // ALL images found in document firstImage: {src, width, height} | null // first image found, or null } ``` Key notes: - `slicedText` is always `<= 215` chars (plus `...`), never longer. - `firstImage` is used everywhere as the card thumbnail — it is just `images[0]`. - On any parse error or empty/non-string input, returns the same shape with empty/null defaults. - The server copy is strictly a utility — it is not used in any endpoint response; it's only called inside `uploadService.js` to extract `wholeText` for embedding generation (see item 3). --- ### 3. `uploadService.js` — What Happens at Write Time File: `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/server/services/uploadService.js` There are two write paths: **create** (`uploadJournalContentService`, line 223) and **update** (`updateJournalService`, line 368). Both paths do the following identically: ```js const parseData = parseTextContentSafely(resolvedContent); // calls server ParseContent const embeddingBody = parseData.wholeText || ''; const embeddings = await GenerateEmbeddings(resolvedTitle, embeddingBody); ``` Then insert/update into `journals` with this payload: ```js { user_id, title, post_type, content, // raw Lexical JSON stored as-is embeddings // pgvector embedding } ``` **What is NOT stored at write time:** `preview_text`, `thumbnail_url`, `slicedText`, or `firstImage.src`. The server already runs `ParseContent` at write time (to feed the embedding), but **it discards everything except `wholeText`**. The thumbnail and preview text are thrown away and re-derived on the client on every render. This means there is a clear, low-risk hook already in place: at line 261 and line 418, `parseData.firstImage?.src` and `parseData.slicedText` are already available — they just are not being persisted. --- ### 4. Journals Table Schema — No Preview/Thumbnail Columns None of the SQL files contain a `CREATE TABLE journals` statement (the table predates all migration files in `server/sql/`). Searching all SQL files for `preview_text`, `thumbnail_url`, `cover_image`, or `preview_image` returns **zero matches**. The known columns added via migrations are: | Source file | Columns added | |---|---| | `scalability_fixes.sql` | `cached_reaction_count`, `cached_comment_count`, `cached_bookmark_count` | | `scalability_fixes.sql` | `content jsonb` (referenced in function), `repost_source_journal_id uuid` | | `hottest_tracker.sql` | indexes only | | `semantic_search.sql` | `embeddings vector(...)` | **No `preview_text` or `thumbnail_url` column exists.** This is the gap the egress optimization would need to fill. --- ### 5. All Components That Use `ParseContent` (9 call-sites across 8 files) | File | Fields Used | |---|---| | `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/ProfilePostCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` (used twice: list view + grid view) | | `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/ProfilePostCards/VisitedProfilePostCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` (used twice: list view + grid view) | | `client/src/components/Bookmarks/Bookmarks.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/HomePage/explore/ExplorePage.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` (called twice: hero card + runner cards) | | `client/src/components/HomePage/explore/InterestSections.jsx` | `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | | `client/src/components/Notifications/UnreadNotificationCard.jsx` | `wholeText`, `slicedText`, `firstImage.src` | **Total: 8 consuming components** (plus `parseData.js` itself = 9 files in the grep results). Every single consumer uses all three of the expensive derived values: thumbnail, preview text, and whole text. --- ### Summary for Egress Optimization The situation is very clean: 1. The `content` column (full Lexical JSON, potentially many KB) is fetched from the DB and shipped to every client for every card in every feed — solely so the client can call `ParseContent()` and extract three small strings. 2. The server already calls `ParseContent` at write time (lines 255-261 and 412-418) — it just discards `slicedText` and `firstImage.src` after using `wholeText` for embeddings. 3. No `preview_text` or `thumbnail_url` columns exist yet on the `journals` table. 4. All 8 consuming components use the same three fields (`slicedText`, `firstImage.src`, `wholeText`). Adding two DB columns (`preview_text varchar(215)` and `thumbnail_url text`) and populating them at write time would allow the feed queries to stop selecting `content` entirely for list views — eliminating the dominant source of egress.
